solution.answer: $H_0 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_1 \cong \mathbb{Z}^3,\quad H_2 \cong \mathbb{Z}^3,\quad H_3 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_n = 0 \text{ for } n \geq 4.$
steps: We know $H_*(T^2) = (\mathbb{Z}, \mathbb{Z}^2, \mathbb{Z}, 0, \ldots)$. Apply Künneth to $T^3 = T^2 \times S^1$.
Since all homology groups are free, Tor terms vanish: $H_k(T^3) = \bigoplus_{p+q=k} H_p(T^2) \otimes H_q(S^1)$.
$H_q(S^1) = \mathbb{Z}$ for $q = 0, 1$ and $0$ otherwise. So: $H_k(T^3) = H_k(T^2) \oplus H_{k-1}(T^2)$.
